rulings.Case Title: WAN FEN TAN VS. RICARDO IAN SUMMERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 6, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANTs RICARDO SUMMERS and ALEJANDRO GOMEZ' MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES DENIED. Request for costs (and attorney's fees as part of costs) is untimely per CRC 8.278(c)(1). Request for attorney's fees based on "common benefit" theory is premature. The parties/referee shall file a motion addressing the propriety of the continued referee appointment and distribution of funds held by the referee (including payment for referee services) in light of the Court of Appeal decision and order denying motion for summary adjudication. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE RAYMOND SCOTT ET AL VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, NA. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 6, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A., SUCCESSOR BY MERGER's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Plaintiff's claims are barred by judicial estoppel. Plaintiff failed to disclose this asset in his bankruptcy filing and obtained benefits of bankruptcy laws protections. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE RAYMOND SCOTT ET AL VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, NA. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 6, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A., SUCCESSOR BY MERGER's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT moot. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DEVLIN/MCNALLEY CONSTRUCTION, INC. VS. MISSION HUB, LLC ET AL

rulings.Case Title: LISA GAUTIER ET AL VS. EDWARD S. YEE MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: For Plaintiff'S Application Fro Preliminary Injunctionand Associated Briefing
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 6, 2019 line 4. For Plaintiff'S Application For Preliminary Injunction and Associated Briefing The Court will issue an order requiring both sides to preserve the status quo, i.e. to be enjoined from interfering with the current condition of the elevator and the easement it is located on unless a valid permit is obtained. Plaintiff to prepare order. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MOISES MONGALO ET AL VS. WALTER WORSHAM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 6, 2019 line 5. DEFENDANT MARSHALL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT continued to April 12, 2019. By no later than March 29, 2019 Plaintiff shall file a supplemental brief listing any cases that are factually similar to the case at bar, specifically a dismissal of an action without prejudice in the face of a pending ANTI-SLAPP followed by filing of a new complaint, including the same causes of action and excluding "slappable" allegations. Plaintiff may accompany each citation by a one paragraph (not to exceed 20 lines) explanation/argument addressing why the case is on point and factually similar. If Plaintiff fails to locate any such cases, the supplemental brief should state so. By no later than April 8, 2019 Defendant may file a supplemental response brief addressing the cases cited by Plaintiff, limiting the argument to one paragraph (not to exceed 20 lines) per case. = (501/CFH)
